title: Portable fluid level monitoring device and method
abstract: Disclosed are devices and methods for determining a fluid level of and the amount of fluid remaining in a container. A portable measuring device can include a housing, a sensor, and a measurement system. The sensor can be supported by the housing and can detect a fluid level of the container. The measurement system can be configured to receive information including a measurement of the container, to determine an amount of fluid in the container based on a measurement of the container and a detected fluid level of the container, and to output the amount of fluid in the container and geometric information of the container. A method can include supplying such a device, placing the device proximal to or in abutment with and moving the device along an outer surface of the container, providing a signal to a user, and outputting the amount of fluid in a container.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A non-intrusive portable liquid product measuring device comprising: a housing; a sensor supported by the housing and configured to detect one or more fluid levels of a container; and a measurement system configured to receive information indicative of a geometric measurement of the container, determine a volume amount of fluid in the container based on the geometric measurement of the container and the one or more detected fluid levels of the container, and output the volume amount of fluid in the container. 2. The device of claim 1 further comprising a signal unit for indicating a detection of a fluid level of the container. 3. The device of claim 1 or 2 wherein the measurement system comprises a graduated measuring instrument for determining one or more distances, including a distance between the sensor and a reference point of the container. 4. The device of any preceding claim wherein the measurement system comprises a non- contact distance sensor configured to determine one or more distances, including a distance between the sensor and a reference point of the container. 5. The device of any preceding claim wherein the measurement system comprises a measuring wheel and is configured to determine one or more distances, including a distance traveled by the measuring wheel from a reference point of the container to a position of the sensor. 6. The device of any preceding claim further comprising a read unit configured to read a marker on the container. 7. The device of claim 6 wherein the marker comprises information indicative of a geometric measurement of the container to be used by the measurement system. 8. The device of any preceding claim further comprising a storage unit configured to receive and store the output from the measurement system. 9. The device of claim 8 wherein the storage unit is further configured to store time, date, location, and calibration data from the device. 10. The device of claim 8 or 9 further comprising a display unit configured to display information from the device. 11. The device of any preceding claim wherein the sensor is selected from the group consisting of a capacitance sensor, LIDAR sensor, RADAR sensor, vision sensor, camera- based sensor, and an ultrasonic sensor. 12. The device of any preceding claim wherein the housing is attachable to the container and wherein the device is configured to be attached to a reference point of the container. 13. The device of any preceding claim wherein the device further comprises a motion system configured to move the device along a track and wherein the device is placed proximal to or in abutment with an outer surface of the container. 14. The device of any preceding claim wherein the device communicates via one or more wireless connections. 15. The device of any preceding claim wherein the measurement system determines the volume amount of fluid in the container using cloud computing. 16. The device of any preceding claim wherein the output from the measurement system is used to generate analytics indicative of a rate of use of fluid in one or more containers. 17. A method for determining an amount of fluid in a container having a fluid level, the method comprising: supplying a non-intrusive portable liquid product measuring device comprising: a housing, 

 a sensor supported by the housing and configured to detect one or more fluid levels of a container, and a measurement system configured to receive information indicative of a geometric measurement of the container, determine a volume amount of fluid in the container based on the geometric measurement of the container and the one or more detected fluid levels of the container, and output the volume of fluid in the container; placing the measuring device proximal to or in abutment with an outer surface of the container; moving the measuring device along a vertical distance of the container; providing a signal to a user that the measuring device has determined the fluid level of the container; and outputting the volume of fluid in a container based on the geometric measurement of the container and the one or more detected fluid levels of the container. 18. The method of claim 17 further comprising any of recording, storing, or displaying measuring device information including the output from the measurement system and wherein moving the measuring device along the vertical distance of the container comprises moving the measuring device between a position above the fluid level of the container and a position below the fluid level of the container. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18 wherein the measuring device further comprises a motion system configured to move the measuring device along a track, wherein the measuring device is placed proximal to or in abutment with an outer surface of the container, and wherein moving the measuring device along the vertical distance of the container comprises the motion system moving the measuring device along the track. 20. A method of monitoring an amount of fluid in a container having a fluid level, the method comprising: supplying one or more non-intrusive portable liquid product measuring devices comprising: a housing, a sensor supported by the housing and configured to detect one or more fluid levels of a container, and 

 a measurement system configured to receive information indicative of a geometric measurement of the container, determine a volume amount of fluid in the container based on the geometric measurement of the container and the one or more detected fluid levels of the container, and output the volume of fluid in the container; affixing the one or more measuring devices to predetermined reference locations along a vertical distance of the container; detecting a change in fluid level via the one or more measuring devices; and determining the amount of fluid remaining in the container based on the geometric measurement of the container and the one or more detected fluid levels of the container. 21. The method of claim 20 further comprising: recording measuring device information including any of the information received by the measurement system, the output of the measurement system, or which of the one or more measuring devices has determined a fluid level of the container, storing recorded measuring device information in a storage unit; and providing a signal to a user that the measuring device has determined the fluid level of the container. 22. The method of claim 20 or 21 further comprising affixing a measuring device to a reference point that indicates when a container should be replaced and wherein providing the signal comprises signaling when said measuring device has determined the fluid level of the container. 23. The method of any of claims 20-22 further comprising: displaying any of the measuring device information and calculating a metric indicating a usage rate of the container. 24. The use of the device of any of claims 1-16 to detect a fluid level of the container and determine an amount of fluid remaining in the container.
